title: Seeles intention for Eva 01 and Kaworu
display_title: Seeles intention for Eva 01 and Kaworu
wikitext:
[]: Seeles intention for Eva 01 and Kaworu             Quote Post by Guy » Fri Nov 27, 2009 9:31 pm Seele mentions while Kaworu descends to Lilith that they wanted Eva 01 to do something for them. But are disappointed with the outcome of what does happen. (The Eva killing Kaworu and all). So what did they want him to do? Could it be that it has something to do with the Evas importance as the only one that has Lilith/angel inside of her because of the s2 engine? I assume they aren't aware that Liliths soul is in Rei. If we go by the manga Gendo says the instrumentality would have been incomplete without her soul anyway but by Gendo saying this it means that they WERE trying to commence third impact by using Kaworu. I don't know if the manga is canon enough but I assume Sakamoto knows more than most. So could the plan have been Kaworu=the soul of Adam + Lilith with assumed soul somehow interacting with a s2 engined 01? Since it would have been Lilith and Adam combining it would have caused a non angel instrumentality and perhaps they wanted Shinji to run it. Its not like they minded it when it happened in EoE. I haven't really put it all together i was just wondering if the thought that unit 01 had an s2 engine had anything to do with whatever was supposed to happen in there plans.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Nov 27, 2009 9:43 pm Seele mentions while Kaworu descends to Lilith that they wanted Eva 01 to do something for them. But are disappointed with the outcome of what does happen. (The Eva killing Kaworu and all).

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Nov 27, 2009 10:30 pm My own opinion on this is that SEELE fully intended Kaworu to fail in his attempt. Their intention was for Unit-01 to stop him; specifically, for Shinji to kill Kaworu. SEELE already know that Shinji has strong quams about harming another person from his refusal in #18. I'd wager that, having lost the Lance in #22', and being forced to use Unit-01 instead of Lilith, they intended to put as much psychological pressure on its pilot as they could. Consider Asuka's long sojourn, and the intelligence divisions "failure" to find here. Could this have been part of SEELE's pressuring of Shinji? Moreover, what about Ritsuko's trip to Terminal Dogma in #23'. She seemed especially keen that Shinji be shown all the secrets of that place. Was she under orders from SEELE to try and break the pilot of Unit-01 mentally? Kaworu's planned demise could have been the culmination of a long series of psychological pressures that SEELE applied to Shinji. if so, it succeeded enormously, with Kaworu's death leaving Shinji all but catatonic in EoE. SEELE may have left little to chance, ensuring that if Unit-01 was used to initiate Third Impact, then the pilot would make his choice to their liking.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 28, 2009 12:06 am Moreover, what about Ritsuko's trip to Terminal Dogma in #23'. She seemed especially keen that Shinji be shown all the secrets of that place. Was she under orders from SEELE to try and break the pilot of Unit-01 mentally?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 28, 2009 12:30 am Episode 23' wrote: Shinji (OFF): Yes, hello? RITSUKO (PHONE - OFF): Just listen to me. I've released the restraints placed on you. You should be able to go outside now. Shinji: Miss Ritsuko? ***** Misato (OFF): It's no use, not without my pass. RITSUKO: I see. It's Kaji's handiwork, isn't it? Misato: You're going to let me see the secret in there with my own eyes. RITSUKO: Fine, but he's coming too. Misato: Fine.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 28, 2009 1:18 am Bah, my memory's crapping out on me, I could've sworn the dialog was the other way around. Well, maybe Ritsuko felt like showing him just to be a bitch, and because he's Gendo's kid. That's kind of how she rolls. You fucked up my family, I'll fuck up yours (then kill you).

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Sat Nov 28, 2009 1:45 am AuraTwilight wrote: Bah, my memory's crapping out on me, I could've sworn the dialog was the other way around. Well, maybe Ritsuko felt like showing him just to be a bitch, and because he's Gendo's kid. That's kind of how she rolls. You fucked up my family, I'll fuck up yours (then kill you).

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Nov 28, 2009 10:54 am The trouble with the theory that Kaworu's mission was to break Shinji's spirit is that it's difficult to reconcile it with the soldiers' orders to kill the pilots. Even if using Shinji was just a backup plan, they wouldn't issue orders that would undo all the work they put into breaking him. What if their main plan didn't work? They would have made their backup plan unusable. I agree that security knew exactly where Asuka was all along, just as they knew exactly where Shinji was in Ep. 04. But wasn't that Nerv security that found her, not Seele? I doubt if they were conspiring with Seele against Shinji. They probably had orders to leave her alone unless she did something destructive or self destructive.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Nov 28, 2009 11:17 am AuraTwilight wrote: SEELE had no plans to use Unit 01 and Shinji in their scenario. Unit 01 was a replacement for Lilith in the case that she was lost, which she wasn't.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 28, 2009 2:55 pm The trouble with the theory that Kaworu's mission was to break Shinji's spirit is that it's difficult to reconcile it with the soldiers' orders to kill the pilots. Even if using Shinji was just a backup plan, they wouldn't issue orders that would undo all the work they put into breaking him. What if their main plan didn't work? They would have made their backup plan unusable.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Nov 28, 2009 5:30 pm AuraTwilight wrote: The trouble with the theory that Kaworu's mission was to break Shinji's spirit is that it's difficult to reconcile it with the soldiers' orders to kill the pilots. Even if using Shinji was just a backup plan, they wouldn't issue orders that would undo all the work they put into breaking him. What if their main plan didn't work? They would have made their backup plan unusable.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy » Sun Nov 29, 2009 1:07 am It just seems a little far far fetched they would risk Kaowru getting so close to a seed of life just to get killed. Perhaps they wanted him to have a role similar to the MP Evas in 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Nov 29, 2009 4:30 am I'm not sure if you got the point. If they need Shinji for a contingency plan they wouldn't kill him.
